event: The Virgin of the Immaculate Conception
symbols: dove descending — the Holy Spirit overshadowing Mary, preserving her from sin
angels bearing her aloft — the heavenly court honoring the Mother of God
roses below — Mary as the Mystical Rose, beauty without the thorn of sin
small temple or tabernacle — Mary as the Ark of the Covenant, the dwelling place of God
dark swirling clouds — the darkness of sin from which Mary was preserved
hands on chest — Mary's receptivity and submission to God's grace
description: A dramatic, swirling composition showing the Virgin Mary standing on dark billowing clouds, her hands pressed to her chest, wearing a red and gold bodice beneath a vast dark blue-green mantle. A white dove descends from above in a burst of light. Four angels in various poses surround and support her amid turbulent clouds. Below at the lower left, a small classical temple or tabernacle sits on the ground, and roses and lilies are visible at the lower right. The composition is intensely dynamic with spiraling movement.
theological_description: The Immaculate Conception — the doctrine that Mary was preserved from original sin from the moment of her conception — is here depicted with explosive visual energy. Mary stands between heaven and earth, the unique creature in whom God prepared a fitting dwelling for His Son. The dove of the Holy Spirit descending recalls the Annunciation (Luke 1:35) but also the primordial Spirit hovering over the waters of creation (Genesis 1:2), suggesting that in Mary a new creation begins. The small temple below symbolizes Mary as the new Ark of the Covenant (Revelation 11:19), the sacred vessel containing the divine presence. The roses and lilies — traditional symbols of Mary's love and purity — grow from the earth that Mary sanctifies by her sinlessness. The swirling, ecstatic composition conveys the cosmic significance of this doctrine: Mary's preservation from sin is not a private privilege but the first act of redemption, the beginning of God's victory over the serpent promised in Genesis 3:15.

scripture_refs.reason: Hail, full of grace — the angelic greeting that the Church reads as affirming Mary's sinlessness
The woman whose seed will crush the serpent — Mary as the New Eve preserved from the serpent's power
The woman clothed with the sun — the heavenly vision of Mary in glory
You are altogether beautiful, my love; there is no flaw in you — applied to Mary's sinlessness

_meta.title: The Virgin of the Immaculate Conception
